etymology.description: 八 (divide) + 口 (object) = equally dividing objects -> public resources
etymology.interpretation: The character 公(gōng, 'public') is traditionally thought of as representing the opposite of 'private'. While some suggest that 八 represents deviation, this modern interpretation sees 八 as division and 口 as a generic object, together symbolizing the distribution of resources into communal or public property.

examples.chinese: 我们应该尊重公共设施(我們應該尊重公共設施)。
这个公园很受当地人的欢迎(這個公園很受當地人的歡迎)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège gōngyuán hěn shòu dāngdì rén de huānyíng.
examples.english: This park is very popular among locals.

examples.chinese: 她在一家公共图书馆工作(她在一家公共圖書館工作)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài yījiā gōnggòng túshūguǎn gōngzuò.
examples.english: She works at a public library.
